danger_signals.visual_form: Large winged demon figure displayed at top of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; signals incoming maze wall movement and danger
Immediate collision hazard; player must avoid or power up to defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Outer boundary of maze shifts direction as Devil points
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; player can be crushed between walls and boundary

danger_signals.visual_form: Dragon character in unpowered state (visual distinction from powered state)
danger_signals.meaning: Player is helpless and cannot interact with threat; vulnerability state

safety_signals.visual_form: Religious cross symbol appearing in maze
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up collectible; grants fire-breathing ability and dot-eating capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Four Bible icons scattered in later maze phases
safety_signals.meaning: Power-up items with same effect as Crosses; placing all four into seal completes level

safety_signals.visual_form: Dedicated maze environment with six boxes and time limit indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Safe period between levels; risk-reward scenario for score/progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated spatial location in maze where Bibles are placed
safety_signals.meaning: Goal location; completing this objective advances to next maze

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hell-themed maze environments; religious iconography inverted as obstacles
Crosses and Bibles serve as alignment symbols; player-collected religious items

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dragon sprite rendered without power effect; cannot interact with collectibles or enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement restricted functionally; cannot defeat minions or progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dragon sprite displays fire-breathing visual effect or enhanced appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can eat dots, defeat minions, and progress toward seal placement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Devil points direction; outer maze boundary shifts visually on screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental walls move; spatial danger zone created between static maze and moving boundary

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Devil exits current maze; transitions to next maze environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All four Bibles removed from world; Devil repositions for next encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dedicated maze variant with six Bonus Box sprites and visible time constraint
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-limited collection scenario; different spatial layout than standard mazes

environmental_storytelling.example: Crosses and Bibles function as collectibles that empower the player; inverts religious symbols as tools against demonic threat
The Devil displayed prominently at top of screen throughout gameplay; establishes hierarchy and constant threat presence
Maze wall movement provides dynamic environmental feedback; player's spatial relationship to boundaries communicates danger state
Green vs. Red dragon variants signal player 1 vs. player 2 without text overlay
Placing Bibles into seal serves as level-completion ritual; visual representation of religious theme integration into mechanics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Points in direction; commands minions to move maze walls; visual gesture at top of screen guides threat direction
Move through maze corridors; unpowered player cannot engage; visual collision hazard
Can be defeated by fire-breathing attack; removed from world state
Movement only; cannot collect dots or defeat enemies; visual helplessness state
Can consume dots, defeat minions, and move toward seal; fire-breathing visual effect active
Shifts in direction signaled by Devil's gesture; creates crushing hazard; dynamic environmental threat

notes: Devil World communicates world-state primarily through visual hierarchy and religious iconography. The Devil's prominent screen position establishes narrative authority; his pointing gesture serves dual function as mechanic cue (maze movement direction) and narrative communication (threat manifestation). Power-up system inverts religious symbols—Crosses and Bibles are offensive/defensive tools against demonic forces, creating thematic resonance between mechanics and visual design. The game's religious content prevented North American release despite Miyamoto/Tezuka design; visual communication of good-vs-evil through iconography is core to world-state expression. Unpowered state creates clear visual/mechanical distinction from powered state, communicating vulnerability without text. Multiplayer color differentiation (green/red) establishes faction identity within cooperative/competitive context.
